Mexico.form_note: Won opening match 2-0 vs South Africa (Quinones 9', Jimenez 67') at the Estadio Azteca. Won with 10 men after Montes red card in 90+2'. Next: vs South Korea June 18.

injuries.note: Achilles tendon rupture; 40-year-old Guillermo Ochoa recalled as replacement
Ruled out injured ahead of the opener
Started vs South Africa; managed minutes
Red card in 90+2' vs South Africa; suspended for the South Korea match (June 18)

key_players.note: Carries the attack into a home World Cup after a strong Premier League season
Genuine penalty-box striker option
17-year-old Liga MX standout, among the youngest players at the tournament
Recalled at 40; set to become only the third player ever (with Messi and Ronaldo) to appear at six World Cups

news: Javier Aguirre trimmed a 55-man preliminary list to the final 26 in late May.
Goalkeeper crisis turned storyline: Malagon's Achilles rupture brings Ochoa's record sixth World Cup.

South Africa.form_note: Lost 0-2 to hosts Mexico in the tournament opener at Estadio Azteca. Finished with 9 men (Sithole red card 49', T. Zwane red card 84' via VAR). Both suspended for next match vs Czech Republic (June 18).

injuries.note: Red card vs Mexico (49'); suspended for the Czech Republic match (June 18)
Red card via VAR vs Mexico (84'); suspended for the Czech Republic match (June 18)

news: First World Cup appearance since hosting in 2010.
Conceded 2 goals and had 2 players sent off vs Mexico in the opener.

South Korea.form_note: Came from 1-0 down to beat Czech Republic 2-1 (Hwang In-beom 67', Oh Hyeon-Gyu 80'; Czech goal L. Krejci 59'). Hwang In-beom played and scored despite ankle concerns. Next: vs Mexico June 18.

injuries.note: Ankle injury; in individual training, a doubt for the opener
Played and scored vs Czech Republic despite pre-tournament ankle concern
Foot injury ruled him out; replaced in squad by uncapped Jo Wi-je

key_players.note: Captain at his fourth World Cup, 33; in sparkling form for LAFC since leaving Tottenham; joint-top Korean World Cup scorer (3)
Primary creator
Defensive cornerstone

news: Hong Myung-bo named the 26 on May 16 with Son the headline.
Group A offers a realistic route to the knockouts.

Canada.form_note: Co-hosts in Group B with Switzerland, Qatar and Bosnia and Herzegovina; final squad named May 29.

injuries.note: ACL tear in the CONCACAF Champions Cup final May 30; replaced by Jayden Nelson on June 9
Confirmed out for the Bosnia opener (June 12) due to hamstring management; head coach Marsch suggests he could return for matchday 2 or 3
Tibia injury rules him out of the tournament

key_players.note: Captain and best player; managing his way back from a hamstring problem
Primary striker for the co-hosts

news: Late injury drama: Flores ruled out on the eve of the tournament, Davies' minutes being managed.
Canada hosts group-stage games in Toronto and Vancouver.

Bosnia and Herzegovina.form_note: In Group B with Canada, Switzerland and Qatar; first World Cup since 2014.

injuries.note: Overcame shoulder injury; confirmed in starting XI for Canada opener (June 12)
Ankle injury; unlikely to feature vs Canada
Muscle problem; managed carefully, should be available
Reserve GK withdrew with injury before the tournament; replaced in squad by Mladen Jurkas

key_players.note: Veteran talisman confirmed to start at 40 despite pre-tournament shoulder concern

news: Returning to the finals after a 12-year absence.
Dzeko confirmed fit and starting vs Canada despite shoulder injury scare.
Reserve GK Hadzikic withdrew injured; Mladen Jurkas added to squad.

Qatar.form_note: In Group B; played Switzerland in their opening fixture. First World Cup qualified on merit after hosting in 2022.
news: Qualified through AFC qualifying after going winless as 2022 hosts.

Switzerland.form_note: Group B with Canada, Qatar and Bosnia and Herzegovina; opened vs Qatar in the first round of fixtures.

key_players.note: Captain and midfield organizer under Murat Yakin
Lead striker

news: Murat Yakin announced the final squad via a social-media campaign, confirmed May 20.
Seen as favorites alongside Canada to advance from a winnable Group B.

Brazil.form_note: Ancelotti's record stands at 7W-2D-3L from 12 games; most recent result a 2-1 win over Egypt. Opener vs Morocco June 13.

injuries.note: ACL rupture and meniscus tear; 7-9 month recovery, likely out for rest of 2026
Biceps femoris tendon rupture; 5-month recovery
Grade-four hamstring injury in April; left off the squad
Grade-two calf strain since May 17; not expected to be ready for the opener vs Morocco June 13
Muscle injury rules him out of the entire World Cup; right-back slot now open (Danilo/Roger Ibanez options)

key_players.note: Arrives in strong form after 61 goal contributions in his sensational 2024-25 season; fit and confident
183 goal involvements in 257 club matches over five seasons (0.79/match) but only 17 in 48 for Brazil (0.36/match) — the gap is the big tactical question
Included at 34 as all-time record scorer; fitness race against calf strain
Returned to fitness in time for the tournament

news: Carlo Ancelotti's first tournament in charge after arriving June 2025 with only four competitive qualifiers under his belt.
Big-name omissions: Joao Pedro, Richarlison, Savinho, Gabriel Jesus, Andrey Santos and 113-cap Thiago Silva all left out; 19-year-old Endrick included.
Injury crisis in attack (Rodrygo, Estevao out; Neymar doubtful) clouds an otherwise all-out attacking project.

Morocco.form_note: 2022 semi-finalists hit by multiple pre-tournament injuries; open against Brazil on June 13 in Group C.

injuries.note: Recovered from a hamstring tear suffered in the Champions League
Groin injury rules him out of the entire World Cup; replaced by Marwane Saadane (Al Fateh)
Knee injury suffered in Norway friendly rules him out of the entire World Cup; replaced by Amine Sbai (Angers)
Shoulder problem from Norway friendly; not thought to be serious but a late call on fitness for June 13

key_players.note: Captain; rated among the world's best right-backs; fit after hamstring scare
Holding-midfield anchor from the 2022 semi-final run
Midfield creator retained from the 2022 generation
Creative attacker expected to start vs Brazil

news: Manager upheaval: Walid Regragui left the post in March 2026; Mohamed Ouahbi (U-20 World Cup-winning coach) now in charge.
Double injury blow: Aguerd (groin) and Ezzalzouli (knee) both ruled out for the tournament — significant defensive and wide-attacking losses.
Mazraoui also a late doubt (shoulder), putting further pressure on the backline vs Brazil.

Haiti.form_note: In Group C with Brazil, Morocco and Scotland; second-ever World Cup and first since 1974.
news: One of the great qualification stories of the cycle given conditions at home; played qualifiers away from Haiti.

Scotland.form_note: In Group C with Brazil, Morocco and Haiti; first World Cup since 1998. Midfield options thinning ahead of June 13 opener vs Haiti.

injuries.note: Knee injury in final friendly vs Curacao; flew to Boston to support teammates as a spectator
Missed Thursday training with an upset stomach; traveled separately from the squad with a doctor; status uncertain for Haiti opener (June 13)
Absent from Thursday training with a knock; fitness to be assessed

key_players.note: Talismanic goal-scoring midfielder; doubt for Haiti opener with stomach illness
Captain and veteran leader

news: Gilmour ruled out with knee injury from final friendly — a significant midfield blow.
McTominay illness adds further midfield concern; coach Steve Clarke due to brief media on June 13 before the Haiti match.

United States.form_note: Hosts open vs Paraguay at SoFi Stadium June 12; fifth-youngest US World Cup roster (avg 26y 332d), 13 returnees from the 2022 round-of-16 squad.

injuries.note: Achilles tendon rupture in April
Ankle injury May 17; only just resumed training — late fitness test ahead of the Paraguay opener (June 12); Miles Robinson and Mark McKenzie in contention

key_players.note: Most-capped player (84) and on-field leader; rested at 2025 Gold Cup to manage load, enters fit and expected to start every game
19 goals and 4 assists in all competitions this season
17 goals, second most in the Championship
19 goals across all competitions in the Eredivisie season

news: Pochettino named the 26 on May 26 after parts of the roster leaked; Gio Reyna in, Diego Luna the notable omission.
Forward line arrives in the best collective form of any US World Cup squad (Balogun, Wright, Pepi all 17+ goals).
Group D with Paraguay, Australia and Turkey.

Paraguay.form_note: Open the tournament against the hosts United States at SoFi Stadium on June 12; first World Cup since 2010.

injuries.note: Thigh injury; will likely miss the USA opener (June 12) — spotted training with the ball showing improvement but not expected to feature
Had a knee issue but returned to full team training; expected to start in midfield

key_players.note: Experienced attacking reference point

news: Qualified comfortably from CONMEBOL on the back of a famously stingy defense.
Star attacker Julio Enciso a fitness doubt for the opener with a thigh issue.

Australia.form_note: In Group D with United States, Turkey and Paraguay; sixth consecutive World Cup appearance.
news: Tony Popovic's side qualified directly through AFC qualifying.

Turkey.form_note: In Group D with United States, Australia and Paraguay; first World Cup since 2002. Open vs Australia at BC Place on June 14.

injuries.note: Fully fit and the focal point of the team; expected to start vs Australia
Calf injury; would be a surprise to see him feature vs Australia on June 14

key_players.note: Creative centerpiece for Turkey's first finals in 24 years; confirmed fit
Second star of the young attacking core; doubt with calf injury
Captain; confirmed fit and expected to start

news: Final squad announced June 2 after a 35-man preliminary list; genuine dark-horse buzz around the young attack.
Yildiz calf injury clouds opener; Guler confirmed fit and will carry attacking responsibility.

Germany.form_note: Julian Nagelsmann's side led by captain Joshua Kimmich; face Curacao, Ivory Coast and Ecuador in a favorable Group E.

injuries.note: Torn adductor in right thigh; misses tournament after a 23-goal-involvement season
Not selected after back surgery and subsequent muscle injury
Thigh muscle tear June 5; replaced in squad by Assan Ouedraogo
Minor calf issue at 40; rested from final friendly as a precaution
Returned to fitness in time

key_players.note: Primary creative force in the final third
Headlines the attacking midfield options alongside Musiala and Sane
Captain and midfield organizer
Among the centre-forward options with Undav and Havertz

news: Goalkeeping situation unsettled: ter Stegen out, 40-year-old Neuer nursing a calf issue before the tournament.
Late squad change: 18-year-old Lennart Karl withdrew injured June 5, replaced by Assan Ouedraogo.

Ivory Coast.form_note: In Group E with Germany, Ecuador and Curacao; reigning AFCON champions (2023).

key_players.note: Hero of the 2023 AFCON triumph

news: African champions pedigree makes them a dangerous third seed in Group E.

Ecuador.form_note: In Group E with Germany, Ivory Coast and Curacao; finalized squad May 31. Qualified comfortably from CONMEBOL behind a very strong defensive record.

key_players.note: One of the Premier League's best holding midfielders, the team's anchor
Cornerstone of the defense

news: Viewed as a sleeper to advance from Group E alongside Germany.

Netherlands.form_note: Unbeaten in qualifying, scoring 27 goals across eight games. Group F opener vs Japan.

injuries.note: ACL rupture in right knee on April 25 playing for Tottenham
Back surgery; confirmed his absence via social media
Ruled out through injury
Ruled out through injury
Left out of the squad (reported out as Timber, De Jong and Depay made it)

key_players.note: Captain and defensive anchor of Koeman's side
Record scorer; recovered from injury in time for the tournament
Midfield linchpin, named in the final 26
Recovered from injury and included

news: Xavi Simons' ACL rupture is the headline absence; defensive depth also hit with De Ligt and De Vrij out.
Ronald Koeman's side seen as the strongest team in Group F ahead of Japan, Sweden and Tunisia.

Japan.form_note: Group F with Netherlands, Sweden and Tunisia; squad named May 15 with two devastating injury omissions on the wings.

injuries.note: Hamstring injury in May vs Wolves; medical staff deemed recovery impossible during the tournament — Moriyasu called it 'a huge blow'
ACL rupture in December
Captain recovered from ankle injury

key_players.note: Japan's one world-class attacker per ratings services; steps up as the main creative threat in Mitoma's absence
Captain and midfield shield, fit again
Key attacking midfielder
Included at 39 for veteran leadership

news: Losing both Mitoma and Minamino strips Japan of its first-choice wide threats; outlook downgraded from dark-horse status.
Moriyasu was visibly emotional announcing Mitoma's omission.

Sweden.form_note: In Group F with Netherlands, Japan and Tunisia; first World Cup since 2018, qualified via the playoffs.

injuries.note: Missed the entire 2025-26 season with a serious knee injury

key_players.note: Headline striker in a potent front pairing
Forms one of the most feared strike duos at the tournament with Isak

news: The Isak-Gyokeres partnership makes Sweden the most dangerous third seed in Group F despite Kulusevski's absence.

Tunisia.form_note: In Group F with Netherlands, Japan and Sweden; qualified unbeaten from African qualifying.
news: One of eight Arab nations at the finals — a record.

Belgium.form_note: Group G favorites against Egypt, Iran and New Zealand; opener vs Egypt June 15. Played Croatia in a pre-tournament friendly.

injuries.note: Just seven Napoli appearances last season; hasn't played since March due to a muscular problem — uncapped Matias Fernandez-Pardo may start instead

key_players.note: Enters the tournament off a disrupted club campaign but remains the creative hub
Primary wide threat in the post-golden-generation attack
Uncapped forward in contention to start up front with Lukaku's fitness in doubt

news: Striker situation is the big worry with Lukaku barely playing since March.
De Bruyne's fitness management is central to Belgium's knockout hopes.

Egypt.form_note: Warm-up results include a 1-1 draw with Spain and a 2-1 loss to Brazil; opener vs Belgium June 15 in Group G.

injuries.note: Recovered from a hamstring tear; available for the opener vs Belgium

key_players.note: Captain; chance to finally leave his mark on a World Cup after injury limited him in 2018
Second star attacker lifting Egypt's ceiling

news: New head coach Hossam Hassan leads Egypt into a group where second place behind Belgium is the target.
One of a record eight Arab nations at this World Cup.

Iran.form_note: In Group G with Belgium, Egypt and New Zealand; fourth consecutive World Cup appearance.

key_players.note: Veteran focal point of the attack

news: Faces Egypt in the group finale with second place likely on the line.

New Zealand.form_note: Oceania's representative in Group G with Belgium, Egypt and Iran; first World Cup since 2010.

key_players.note: Captain and Premier League-proven striker

news: Secured the OFC's first-ever direct qualification slot.

Spain.form_note: Most likely winners in simulation models (16.1%); recent warm-ups include a 1-1 draw with Egypt despite dominant possession; Yamal rested vs Peru in final friendly.

injuries.note: Fifth metatarsal fracture requiring surgery
ACL tear suffered in February
Left hamstring injury from April 22 (Barcelona); coach De la Fuente expects him fit for the opener vs Cape Verde June 15
Late-season knock being managed

key_players.note: 25 goals and 20 assists in 2025-26; most assists in La Liga (8); set to make his World Cup debut at 18
Rejuvenated under Hansi Flick; regarded as one of the finest midfielders in the world
First-choice striker option in a fluid front line
Named in the 26 alongside Zubimendi and Fabian Ruiz in a deep midfield

news: Squad contains no Real Madrid players for the first time in World Cup history.
Euro 2024 champions and narrow tournament favorites over France in most odds markets.
Concern raised over chance creation after generating just four chances in the 1-1 warm-up draw with Egypt.

Cape Verde.form_note: World Cup debutants in Group H; open against Spain on June 15.
news: Won African qualifying Group D ahead of Cameroon; third-least populous nation ever at a World Cup.

Saudi Arabia.form_note: In Group H with Spain, Uruguay and Cape Verde; seventh World Cup appearance.

key_players.note: Veteran star and scorer of the famous 2022 winner vs Argentina

news: Herve Renard back at the helm for another tournament cycle.

Uruguay.form_note: Qualified 4th in CONMEBOL (28 pts: 7W-7D-4L, 22 scored, 12 conceded); drew England 1-1 at Wembley in March via a stoppage-time Valverde penalty.

key_players.note: 20 goal involvements in 42 appearances in 2025-26; Uruguay's engine and primary leader
Anchors a defense built on strength and experience
Captain, on the verge of 100 caps
Hasn't played for Al Hilal since February after foreign-player registration squeeze; no international goal in almost two years

news: Marcelo Bielsa leads Uruguay into Group H with Spain, Saudi Arabia and Cape Verde.
Darwin Nunez's lack of club football and two-year international goal drought is the major attacking concern.

France.form_note: Co-favorites with Spain (~16% in market models); concluded warm-ups with a 3-1 win over Northern Ireland.

injuries.note: Achilles tendon rupture in April (slipped during Liverpool's UCL exit vs PSG); recovery extends into late 2026
Knee injury, ruled out for the season
Recovered from thigh/muscle injury that caused him to miss Real Madrid's La Liga run-in; will captain the side

key_players.note: 48 goals and 10 assists in 2025-26 (29 in 24 La Liga matches, 15 in 11 UCL apps); chasing France and World Cup all-time scoring records
Reigning Ballon d'Or holder (35g/14a in 2024-25); just won a second Champions League title vs Arsenal on May 30
Competing for a starting berth in a stacked attack with Doue, Cherki and Thuram
Part of PSG's Champions League-winning core arriving in peak form

news: This tournament is Didier Deschamps' swansong as manager after two consecutive finals; squad motivated to send him off with a third star.
Arguably the deepest roster in the 48-team field, especially in attack.
Group I with Senegal, Norway and Iraq — the standout 'group of death' candidate among top seeds.

Senegal.form_note: Unbeaten in qualifying; recently became the first African side to beat England, winning 3-1 at Wembley. Arguably Africa's best team entering Group I.

key_players.note: Record scorer (55 intl goals); top scorer in qualifying with 5; has confirmed international retirement after the tournament
On loan from Chelsea; behind Kane and Diaz at club level but a potent physical striker at his best
Dynamic all-round midfielder despite a difficult club season

news: Pape Thiaw's golden generation faces France, Norway and Iraq in the toughest-looking group.
Third consecutive World Cup appearance; momentum high after the Wembley win over England.

Iraq.form_note: In Group I with France, Senegal and Norway; first World Cup since 1986, qualified via the playoffs.
news: Rank outsiders in the tournament's toughest group.

Norway.form_note: Perfect qualifying record — eight wins in eight with a Europe-leading 37 goals. First World Cup appearance in 28 years.

key_players.note: 51 goals and 10 assists in 2025-26; scored 16 in qualifying (double any other European player); 55 intl goals at 25
Captain and midfield maestro
Experienced second striker option
Young winger completing a balanced attack with Oscar Bobb

news: The trendy dark-horse pick: drawn into Group I with France, Senegal and Iraq — the marquee group of the tournament.
Haaland's first major tournament; Norway's first since 1998.

Argentina.form_note: Four wins in last five matches with only one goal conceded; beat Iceland 3-0 in final warm-up (Messi penalty). Defending champions, Group J opener vs Algeria June 16.

injuries.note: Soleus muscle injury suffered June 6 in training camp; Marseille captain required late replacement
Required surgery, ruled out of the tournament
Knee injury
Knee injury
Fully recovered from MCL sprain
Overcame a relatively minor hamstring injury; expected to lead the team in the opener

key_players.note: 12 goals and 7 assists in the 2026 MLS season (avg FotMob rating 8.47); turns 39 during tournament, his sixth and final World Cup
First-choice striker; part of an Atletico-heavy attacking contingent (with Almada, N. Gonzalez, G. Simeone)
Named in final squad as a senior forward option
Creative option; one of 17 returning players from the 2022 title-winning squad

news: Squad features 17 of the 26 players who won the 2022 title in Qatar; Argentina aim to become first back-to-back champions since Brazil in 1962.
Considered to have drawn a kind group (Algeria, Austria, Jordan); analysts argue they are underpriced as only sixth favorite on odds boards.
Messi confirmed fit after minor hamstring concern; scored a penalty in the 3-0 warm-up win over Iceland.

Algeria.form_note: In Group J; open against defending champions Argentina on June 16. First World Cup since 2014.

injuries.note: Recovered from a facial injury; first-choice goalkeeper for the opener vs Argentina

key_players.note: Captain and chief creator
Zinedine Zidane's son, choosing Algeria, starts in goal

news: AFCON 2025 winners momentum; the Argentina opener is the marquee tie of Group J.

Austria.form_note: Topped their qualifying group with six wins from eight, finishing two points clear. First World Cup since 1998; opener vs Jordan June 16, then Argentina June 22.

key_players.note: Captain at his first World Cup
All-time record scorer; broke the record during qualification
20-year-old winger; 3 goals in 22 appearances in a productive first PSV season

news: Ralf Rangnick's pressing side is a popular pick to join Argentina out of Group J.

Jordan.form_note: World Cup debutants in Group J with Argentina, Austria and Algeria.

key_players.note: Star winger from the 2023 Asian Cup final run

news: Qualified automatically by finishing second behind South Korea in their AFC group.

Portugal.form_note: Reigning UEFA Nations League champions, ranked fifth in the world; rated the strongest side outside the top five favorites.

key_players.note: At 41 becomes the first man to play six World Cups; already the only player to score at five World Cups; chasing the one trophy that has eluded him
Arrives straight from winning the Champions League with PSG
Champions League winner; core of Portugal's elite midfield
Part of a deep forward group with Trincao and Goncalo Ramos behind Ronaldo
Widely rated the best left-back in the world after PSG's CL triumph

news: Roberto Martinez named the squad May 19; a symbolic squad place was dedicated to the late Diogo Jota.
Reserve keeper Ricardo Velho travels as extra cover.
Group K with Colombia, Uzbekistan and DR Congo — strong favorites to top it.

DR Congo.form_note: In Group K with Portugal, Colombia and Uzbekistan; first World Cup since 1974 (as Zaire). Qualified via the intercontinental playoff.
news: Won the African playoff path then the FIFA intercontinental playoff to reach a first finals in 52 years.

Uzbekistan.form_note: World Cup debutants in Group K; the first Central Asian nation ever at a World Cup. Unbeaten through the AFC second round, qualified with a game to spare.

key_players.note: Centre-back and the squad's highest-profile player
Record scorer leading the line

news: Group K finale vs Colombia likely decides their knockout hopes.

Colombia.form_note: Qualified 3rd in CONMEBOL with 28 points from 18 games; squad announced May 26 by Nestor Lorenzo.

key_players.note: Undisputed leader and Colombia's top scorer in qualifying; key creative force at Bayern
34-year-old talisman left his club May 13 to join camp early; denied retirement rumors
Among the striker options in a deep attacking pool

news: Group K schedule: Uzbekistan in Mexico City, DR Congo in Guadalajara, Portugal in Miami.
Diaz and James were integral throughout the qualifying campaign and headline the 26.

England.form_note: Perfect qualifying campaign — won all eight games without conceding a goal. Opener vs Croatia at AT&T Stadium on June 17.

injuries.note: Hamstring injury from late-April Merseyside derby
Season-ending foot surgery; missed last four months

key_players.note: 36 goals in 31 games as Bayern won the title; European Golden Shoe winner averaging a goal every 66.1 minutes in the Bundesliga; captains England at a record-equalling third World Cup
Handed the No 10 shirt; club form indifferent with injury concerns this season but undroppable
Established No 1 behind a defense that didn't concede in qualifying
Handed a key shirt number in Tuchel's squad reveal

news: Thomas Tuchel's ruthless squad cuts: Cole Palmer, Phil Foden, Trent Alexander-Arnold, Harry Maguire and Adam Wharton all left out — biggest selection story of the build-up.
Ivan Toney and Ollie Watkins named as the back-up strikers to Kane.
ESPN power-rankings panel gave England zero first-place votes amid uncertainty over Tuchel's selections despite the flawless qualifying record.

Croatia.form_note: 2022 third-place finishers arrive with an experienced core; opener vs England June 17. Based in Alexandria, Virginia for the group stage.

injuries.note: Recovered from broken shinbone surgery
Recovered from facial/cheekbone fracture in time for the tournament

key_players.note: At 40 still playing 90 minutes weekly for Milan; record sixth World Cup, 2018 Golden Ball winner
Defensive cornerstone, back fit after shinbone surgery
One of four centurions in the squad alongside Modric, Perisic and Kramaric
Veteran attacking outlet, also past 100 caps

news: ESPN backs the Vatreni to reach the knockout stage from Group L with England, Ghana and Panama.
Croatia's 2026 World Cup jersey was ranked last by The Athletic, prompting a defense from the federation.

Ghana.form_note: In Group L with England, Croatia and Panama; fifth World Cup appearance.

injuries.note: Long-term injury rules out Ghana's biggest star
Long-term injury

key_players.note: Carries the main attacking burden with Kudus out

news: Losing Kudus and Salisu severely dents hopes of escaping Group L.

Panama.form_note: In Group L with England, Croatia and Ghana; second World Cup after 2018.

key_players.note: Midfield orchestrator of the CONCACAF qualifying campaign

news: Topped their CONCACAF final-round group to qualify directly.
